articles.title: Sophie Turner Gets Hurt, Tomb Raider Show Stops Filming
articles.summary: Filming for the new Tomb Raider TV show has stopped because actress Sophie Turner got hurt. She plays Lara Croft, a famous character from a video game. The show is being made for Prime Video. Bosses said Sophie had a small injury, so they paused filming to let her get better. They hope to start again soon. Sophie is known from Game of Thrones and X-Men movies. The show also has other actors like Sigourney Weaver. Lara Croft is a popular hero in games, and this show is based on those games. The injury is not serious, but they are being careful.

articles.title: Ancient Shield Comes Home to Scotland
articles.summary: A very old shield from the Bronze Age has come back to Scotland after over 230 years away. It was found in a peat bog in Beith, North Ayrshire, around 1779 and was kept in London since 1791. Now, it is on loan to the National Museum of Scotland for a special exhibition called "Scotland's First Warriors" opening this summer. The shield is about 60-70cm wide and has decorations and damage from weapons, showing it was used in battle. Experts think it belonged to someone important because it is rare and hard to make. It was buried with other shields, which might have been part of a ceremony for peace. The exhibition will also show other shields found in Scotland.

articles.title: Oil Prices Go Up and Stocks Go Down in Asia
articles.summary: Oil prices went up a lot and stock markets in Asia went down on Monday. This happened because the war between the US, Israel, and Iran is now in its fifth week. The price of Brent crude oil rose above $115 a barrel. In Japan and South Korea, stock markets fell by almost 3%. Iran-backed groups attacked Israel over the weekend, and Iran threatened more strikes. US President Donald Trump said he might take oil from Iran. A shipping expert warned that the war could make oil and food prices go up even more, affecting poor countries. This conflict is causing big changes in global markets.

articles.title: Jannik Sinner Wins Big Tennis Tournaments
articles.summary: Jannik Sinner is a tennis player who made history by winning two big tournaments in a row: Indian Wells and the Miami Open. This is called the 'Sunshine Double'. He won all his matches without losing a set, which is very hard to do. In the Miami final, he beat Jiri Lehecka 6-4 6-4. Sinner is now the world number two and is catching up to Carlos Alcaraz, who is the top player. Sinner has won 34 sets in a row at Masters events, joining greats like Novak Djokovic and Rafael Nadal. He and Alcaraz are the best players right now, and they keep trying to beat each other. Sinner improved his serve, hitting many aces and rarely losing his service games. This helped him win in Miami and shows he is getting better.

articles.title: Actor Paapa Hopes TV Show Helps People Talk About Baby Loss
articles.summary: Paapa Essiedu is an actor in a TV show called Babies. The show is about a couple named Lisa and Steven who are trying to have a baby but face a sad event called pregnancy loss. This means they lose the baby before it is born. Paapa wants the show to help people talk about this topic because many feel shy or alone. He worked with doctors and midwives to learn about it since he is not a parent. Another actor, Siobhán, also talked to women who had similar experiences. The show's writer, Stefan, made it honest and caring. The goal is to break the silence so people can share their feelings and not feel isolated.

articles.title: Why Are Big Tech Companies Cutting Jobs and Blaming AI?
articles.summary: Big tech companies like Google, Amazon, and Meta are cutting many jobs, and they are saying it's because of artificial intelligence (AI). AI is a type of technology that helps machines think and do tasks like humans. These companies say AI lets them do more work with fewer people. For example, Meta's boss Mark Zuckerberg thinks AI will change how we work by 2026. Jack Dorsey from Block says AI tools help smaller teams work better. But some people are skeptical and think companies might be using AI as an excuse to save money or make shareholders happy instead of just blaming costs. This is happening now at many tech firms around the world.
